translations.source: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.reference: Taarifa ya kusherehekea maadhimisho ya miaka 50 ya HRP: Swideni na Uingereza na Ireland kaskazini Ili kukamata ari ya wajumbe wa Kamati ya Sera na Uratibu (PCC) wanaosherehekea miaka 50 ya HRP na mafanikio yake mengi, Sweden na Uingereza na Ireland Kaskazini walisoma maelezo yafuatayo ya shukurani na pongezi katika mkutano wa 36 wa HRP PCC. Kizazi chetu kimeshuhudia maendeleo zaidi katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote vilivyotangulia kwa pamoja na kushuhudia mabadiliko kadhaa ya kiakili, kiteknolojia, kiafya na kijamii yakiongozwa na mapinduzi ya uzazi wa mpango na kufuatiwa na maendeleo ya afya ya uzazi,ubunifu wa afya vilevile haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto kubwa katika uga wa SRHR zinaendelea, na, bilashaka, baadhi ya maendeleo leo yanahojiwa tena. Tunawapongeza wafanyakazi wa HRP, washirika na waungaji mkono kwa mafanikio mengi hadi sasa na kutambua hatua kubwa zilizofikiwa katika nusu karne katika kutoa ushahidi unaounga mkono upatikanaji kamili wa usawa wa afya na haki za ngono na uzazi. Tungependa kutaja makundi makuu matatu ya mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa HRP umekuwa na athari inayoonekana kwenye sera ya afya ya umma, viwango, na mazoezi Dunianikote kushughulikia mahitaji yanayoendelea ya watu mbalimbali. Pili,HRP, hasa kupitia muungano wa HRP, imeimarisha taasisi za utafii ndani na nje ya nchi, na imewezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti ambao unahakikisha kwamba utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa SRHR unaweza kuendelea, na kwa hakika kuongezeka, katika siku zijazo. Tatu, katika Dunia iliyokumbwa na matatizo, migogoro na kuongezeka upinzani dhidi ya usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imewezesha uzingatiaji wa haki za kimataifa za wanawake na wasichana na watu walio katika mazingira magumu. HRP inasalia kuwa chombo kikuu ndani ya mfumo wa umoja wa mataifa kwa ajili utafiti katika uzazi wa Binadamu na inaendelea kuwa kiongozi wa kipekee katika afya ya ngono na uzazi Duniani.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Tamko la sherehe kwa kumbukumbu ya miaka 50 ya HRP: Uswidi na Ufalme wa Muungano wa Uingereza na Ireland Kaskazini.

Ili kuonesha roho ya washiriki wa Kamati ya Sera na Uratibu (PCC) wakisherehekea miaka 50 ya HRP na mafanikio yake mengi, Uswidi na Ufalme wa Muungano wa Uingereza na Ireland walisoma taarifa ifuatayo ya shukrani na pongezi katika mkutano wa 36 wa PCC wa HRP.

Kizazi chetu kimeshuhudia maendeleo makubwa katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote vilivyotangulia, na kumeshuhudia mabadiliko kadhaa ya kiakili, kiteknolojia, matibabu na kijamii yaliyoongozwa na mapinduzi ya ulinzi wa uzazi, yafuatiwa na maendeleo katika afya ya uzazi, ubunifu wa afya, pamoja na haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto nyingi katika uwanja wa Afya ya Uzazi na Kingono (SRHR) zinaendelea, na, inaweza kusema, baadhi ya maendeleo yanatiliwa shaka leo.

Tunashukuru wafanyakazi wa HRP, washirika na wafuasi kwa mafanikio mengi hadi sasa, na kutambua hatua kubwa zilizofanywa katika miaka hamsini katika kuzalisha ushahidi unaounga mkono upataji kamili na sawa wa afya ya uzazi na kingono na haki. Tungependa kuainisha makundi matatu makuu ya mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa hali ya juu wa HRP umekuwa na athari halisi katika sera za afya ya umma, viwango na mazoea duniani kote, yakihusu mahitaji yanayoendelea ya idadi tofauti za watu. Pili, HRP, hasa kupitia Muungano wa HRP, imeimarisha taasisi za utafiti ndani ya nchi na kimataifa, na imewezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti unaohakikisha kuwa utafiti wa hali ya juu wa SRHR unaweza kuendelea, na kweli, kuongezeka katika siku zijazo. Tatu, katika ulimwengu unaokumbwa na mgogoro, migogoro na upinzani unaoongezeka wa usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imefanikisha kuheshimiwa kwa haki za kimsingi za wanawake na wasichana na makundi yenye ulinzi. HRP inabaki chombo kikuu ndani ya mfumo wa Umoja wa Mataifa kwa utafiti katika uzazi wa binadamu na inaendelea kuwa kiongozi wa kipekee katika afya ya uzazi na kingono kimataifa.

---

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **PCC:**  Similarly, "PCC" is kept as is.
*   **SRHR:**  Translated as "Afya ya Uzazi na Kingono" (Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights). This is the standard Swahili translation.
*   **"Note of appreciation and congratulations":** Translated as "taarifa ya shukrani na pongezi" - a direct and common phrasing.

*   **Flow and Style:** I've aimed for a formal and professional tone, appropriate for a statement of celebration. I've used sentence structures that are common in Swahili writing.
*   **"Tangible impact":** Translated as "athari halisi" - conveys the idea of a real and noticeable effect.
*   **"Beset by crisis, conflict":** Translated as "unaokumbwa na mgogoro, migogoro" - captures the sense of being affected by these issues.

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific audience and context. If you have more information about who will be reading this, I can refine the translation further.

translations.source: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.reference: HRP na washirika wake wametuleta karibu na ahadi ya mkutano wa kimataifa wa idadi ya watu na maendeleo wa 1994 na wito wake wa kuweka hali za watu binafsi, hususani wanawake na wasichana, katika mhimili wa maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kutoa pongezi na shukurani zetu kwa sekretarieti ya HRP, washirika wake, wafadhili wenza na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo shirikishi na wataalamu kusaidia kuhakikisha juhudi zake zinachangia kuleta mabadiliko kwa wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana na jamii mbalimabali HRP imejitolea kuhudumia. Kwa niaba ya wanachama wa PCC, turuhusu kuthibitisha ushirikiano wetu nanyi ili kuunga mkono mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo mafanikio ya ajenda ya 2030 inategemea.
translations.hypothesis: HRP na washirika wake wametufikisha karibu na ahadi ya Mkutano wa Kimataifa wa Mwaka 1994 wa Popote na Maendeleo, na wito wake wa kuweka haki za watu binafsi, hasa wanawake na wasichana, katika moyo wa maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kuonyesha shukrani na tumaini letu kwa ustaarabu wa HRP, washirika wake, wadhamini, na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo vinavyoshirikiana na wataalamu wanaosaidia kuhakikisha jitihada zake zinafanya tofauti kwa wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana na jumuiya mbalimbali ambazo HRP imejitolea kuwahudumia. Kwa niaba ya wajumbe wa PCC, ruhusu sisi kuthibitisha upya ushirika wetu na nyinyi ili kusaidia mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo mafanikio ya Ajenda ya 2030 yanategemea.

translations.source: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.reference: WHO inazindua mpango mpya wa kuboresha utayari wa janga Ili kusaidia nchi kujiandaa vyema kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko ya siku sijazo, WHO imezindua mpango mpya leo ambao unatoa muongozo kuhusu upangaji jumuishi wa kukabiliana na vimelea vyovyote vya upumuaji kama vile mafua au virusi vya Korona. Mpango mpya wa kujitayarisha na ustahimilivu kwa mpango wa vitisho vinavyoibuka, au PRET, inajmuisha zana na mbinu za hivi punde zaidi za kujifunza pamoja na kuchukua hatua za pamoja zilizoainishwa wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za hivi karibuni za afya ya Umma. Kupitia mpango huo, WHO itatumia mbinu ya maambukizi kuziongoza nchi katika upangaji wa janga hilo, ikizingatiwa kwamba nafasi na uwezo mkubwa ni wa wakaida miongoni mwa makundi ya vimelea vya magonjwa. PRET inajibu mwito wa muongozo za kiufundi na usaidizi kwa ajili ya kukuza na kuimarisha utayari jumuishi na muitikio, kama ilivyoainishwa katika maazimio ya baraza la afya ulimwenguni. Janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za kiafya zimeonyesha kuwa nchi zinahitaji kuwa tayari kukabiliana na matishio ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, na mipango mahususi ya kujitayarisha mikononi na uratibu bora na ushirikiano na sekta nyingine kama vile kilimo. "Shughuli za kujitayarisha, kuzuia, na kukabiliana hazipaswi kuwa eneo la sekta ya afya pekee", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kama vile hali za dharura za kiafya zinavyoathiri katika sekta nyingi, ndivyo pia juhudi zetu za kujitayarisha na kukabiliana nazo zienee katika sekta, taaluma na viini vya magonjwa. Pia ni muhimu kwamba ushirikishwaji wa jamii na usawa ni kitovu cha jamii zetu, hususani kwa wale watu waliotengwa na walio hatarini zaidi". PRET ni mageuzi katika mkabala wa WHO wa kujitayarisha kwa janga kupitia utumiaji wa lenzi ya maambukizi, badala ya kuzingatia magonjwa mahususi.

translations.source: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.reference: WHO itaendelea kuandaa na kusambaza muongozo kuhusu magonjwa maalumu inapohitajika. Uzinduzi wa mpango huo unafanyika katika siku ya mwisho ya mikutano na zaidi ya watu 120 wanaowakilisha nchi wanachama, washirika na sekretarieti ya WHO, kufafanua mustakabali ujao wa PRET, ikijumuisha maendeleo ya mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya janga la vimelea. Sehemu ya kwanza ya mpango wa PRET inaangazia vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, ikiwa ni pamojana mafua, virusi vya Korona, virusi vya kupumua vya syncytial. Kwa kuzingatia janga la UVIKO-19 linaloendelea na tishio linalowezekana la mafua ya ndege, sehemu hii itawezesha nchi kukagua, kupima na kusasisha juhudi zao za kupanga janga la kupumua ili kuhakikisha kuwa zina nafasi na uwezo wa kufanya kazi. Mchakato unaendelea ili kutambua kundi la linalofuata la vimelea vya magonjwa, kama vile Arboviruses, kushughulikiwa chini ya mpango huu. Hii itafuata vipaumbele vilivyoainishwa kupitia mapendekezo kumi ya kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya dharura ya afya, majibu, na ustahimilivu (HEPR). Mpango wa PRET unaoleta enzi mpya ya kujitayarisha kwa janga na inawakilisha mageuzi ya shughuli za msingi za WHO ili kusaidia nchi wanachama wote katika kuimarisha utayari wa dharura wa afya, kuzuia, na kukabiliana na nafasi na uwezo. Pia mpango huo unaweza kutumika kutekeleza malengo na masharti ya makubaliano ya janga la ugonjwa ambao kwa sasa unajadiliwa na nchi wanachama wa WHO. Kufuatia mijadala na mazungumzo miongoni mwa washiriki wa mkutano, WHO inawaalika washikadau kwenye wito huu wa kimataifa kuchukua hatua ili kuendeleza utyarishaji wa vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua. Wito kwa hatua.

translations.source: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.reference: WHO, nchi wanachama, na washirika wanafanya kazi pamoja kutambua na kutekeleza hatua za kipaumbele ili kuimarisha utayari wa kitaifa, kikanda na kimataifa kwa magonjwa ya milipuko na matishio yanayoibuka ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Tunatambua jukumu la jamii na sekta zote, na haja ya kuimarishwa na kuendelea kwa kasi ili kumaliza mzunguko wa hofu na kutekelezwa ambao ni wa kawaida sana katika kujiandaa na kukabiliana na janga. Tunakumbuka mafunzo kutoka kwa majanga na magonjwa ya milipuko ya hapo awali, hususani yale yanayosababishwa na magonjwa ya kupumua. Vimelea hivi vina na vitaendelea kuwa tishio kubwa la kimataifa lenye uwezekano wa kusababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, kuelemea mifumo ya afya, na kudhoofisha uchumi wa Dunia, na kuzidisha ukosefu wa usawa, ambao upo katika upatikanaji wa zana zote mbili za kuzuia magonjwa ya mlipuko na huduma za afya kwa watu wote. Huu ni wito wa kuchukua hatua ili kuharakisha kujitayarisha kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko na vitisho vinavyoibuka Duniani. Kujiandaa kwa ufanisi kunategemea mipango thabiti na hatua zilizoratibiwa. Ahadi ya pamoja ya hatua zifuatazo itaona maendeleo yaliyofikiwa ifikapo Desemba 2025 kama yatakavyo fafanuliwa katika mfumo wa ufuatiliaji wa (kujiandaa na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyojitokeza (PRET): Kusasisha mipango ya kujitayarisha ambayo inathibitisha vitendo vya kipaumbele na ambavyo imezingatia mafunzo kutoka kwa matukio ya zamani. Kutambua hatari inayoletwa na vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, kupanga janga la vimelea vya upumuaji kwa kuzingatia mada zilizoainishwa katika Moduli ya PRET #1. Kupanga kipaumbele kwa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji

translations.source: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Kuongeza muunganisho kati ya washikadau katika upangaji maandalizi ya janga kupitia uratibu na ushirikiano wa kimfumo. Hii inajumuisha kujenga mifumo yenye usawa; kufanya mazoezi ya viungo; Na kushiriki taarifa kuhusu mazoea mazuri, changamoto na fursa. Kujitolea kwa uwekezaji endelevu, ufadhili na ufuatiliaji wa utayari wa janga kwa kuzingatia mapengo yaliyo ainishwa wakati w magonjwa ya mlipuko na milipuko iliyopita. Muitikio wa janga la UVIKO-19 umeonyesha kile kinachoweza kufikiwa kwa kujitolea kisiasa, ushirikishwaji wa jamii na ufadhili. Kiini cha kazi hii ni kuhakikisha usawa kuwa tayari kwa janga lijalo pamoja. Hatua ya jamuii nzima inahitajika ili kufanya maendeleo yaliyoainishwa katika wito huu wa hatua. Utekelezaji unapaswa kuimarisha uimara wa jamii, kudumisha , kuendeleza, na kujenga juu ya mifumo ya kawaida; na kuongeza uwezo mpana zaidi wa kujiandaa na kukabiliana na Dharura. Wito huu wa kuchukua hatua ulitolewa katika maandalizi na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyoibuka (PRET): mkutano wa kimataifa wa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji ya baadae uliofanyika tarehe 24-26 Aprili 2023 huko Jeniva, Uswisi.

Katikati ya kazi hii ni kuhakikisha usawa ili tuwe tayari kwa janga lijalo pamoja.  Hatua za jamii nzima zinahitajika ili kufanikisha maendeleo yaliyoainishwa katika Mwaliko huu wa Hatua.  Utekelezaji unapaswa kuimarisha uimara wa jumuiya; kudumisha, kuendeleza, na kujenga juu ya mifumo ya kawaida; na kutumia uwezo mpana wa ulinzi na majibu ya dharura.  Mwaliko huu wa Hatua ulitolewa katika Mkutano wa Ulinzi na Usimamivu wa Tishio Zinazoibuka (PRET): Mkutano wa Kimataifa wa Janga la Magonjwa ya Kupumua ya Baadaye uliofanyika tarehe 24-26 Aprili 2023 mjini Geneva, Uswizi.

translations.source: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.reference: WHO inapendekeza kuongezeka kwa utekelezaji wa zana zinazopendekezwa za kukabiliana na Malaria Kuadhimisha siku ya Malaria Duniani, shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) linatoa wito wa kuongezwa kwa utekelezaji wa afua mpya na zilizopo ili kuokoa misha kutokana na Malaria. Karibu watoto milioni 1.5 walio katika hatari kubwa ya ugonjwa na kifo kutokana na Malaria nchini Ghana, Kenya na Malawi sasa wamepokea dozi yao ya kwanza ya chanjo ya kwanza ya Malaria, RTS,S/AS 01 (RTS,S), shukrani kwa mpango unaoendelea wa majaribio unaoratibiwa na WHO. Majaribio ya chanjo ya Malaria, ilyozinduliwa mwaka 2019, inaongeza usawa katika ufikiaji wa kuzuia Malaria kwa walio hatarini zaidi na wanaookoa maisha. Kama itatekelezwa kwa mapana, WHO inakadiria kuwa chanjo ya Malaria inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi kwa maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. "Tunazo zana za kukabiliana na ugonjwa wa Malaria, kifurushi cha afua ambacho kinajumuisha udhibiti wa Vekta, Dawa za kuzuia, upimaji na matibabu", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Hawa wameunganishwa na chanjo salama na yenye ufanisi ya Malaria, ambayo, inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. Kwa uwekezaji endelevu na juhudi zilizoongezwa kufikia walio hatarini zaidi, kutokomeza Malaria katika nchi nyingi kunawezekana. "Siku ya Malaria Duniani 2023 inaadhimisha chini ya kauli mbiu "wakati wa kutokomeza Malaria: Wekeza, Vumbua, tekeleza". Ndani ya Mada hii, WHO inahimiza utekelezaji bora zaidi wa zana na mikakati iliyopo ya kuzuia, kuchunguza na kutibu Malaria, hasa miongoni mwa watu waliotengwa.

translations.source: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.reference: Kulingana na ripoti ya hivi karibuni ya ugonjwa wa Malaria Duniani, iliyochapishwa mnamo Desemba 2022, kulikuwa na visa vipya milioni 247 vya Malaria mwaka 2021. WHO kanda ya Afrika inaendelea kubeba mzigo mzito zaidi wa ugonjwa huo ikichukua wastani wa 95% ya visa vyote vya Malaria (milioni 234) na 96% ya vifo vote (593,000) mnamo 2021. Karibu 80% ya vifo vya Malaria katika kanda ya Afrika vilikuwa miongoni mwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Mikakati na zana mpya Nchi zimepiga hatua katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria kwa watu walio katika hatari zaidi. Licha ya mafanikio fulani, watu wengi walio katika hatari kubwa ya Malaria bado wanakosa huduma zinazoweza kuzuia, kugundua na kutibu ugonjwa huo. Changamoto katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria zimeongezeka, hususani huko Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara, janga la UVIKO-19, kuunganisha migogoro ya kibinadamu, vikwazo vya fedha, mifumo dhaifu ya ufuatiliaji, na kupungua kwa ufanisi wa zana kuu za kupambana na malaria. Kushughulikia matishio haya na kusaidia nchi katika kujenga programu zinazostahimili ugonjwa wa Malaria, WHO hivi karibuni ilichapisha mikakati na mifumo mipya, ikijumuisha: Mkakati mpya wa kudhibiti ukinzani wa dawa za Malaria barani Afrika; Mpango mpya wa kukomesha kuenea kwa Anopheles stephensi katika mazingira ya mijini;

Eneo la Afrika la WHO bado linabeba mzigo mzito zaidi wa ugonjwa huu – likihesabiwa kama takriban 95% ya visa vyote vya malaria (milioni 234) na 96% ya vifo vyote (593,000) mwaka 2021. Karibu 80% ya vifo vya malaria katika Eneo la Afrika vilikuwa kati ya watoto walio chini ya umri wa miaka mitano.

Mbinu na zana mpya. Nchi zimefanya maendeleo kidogo katika kupanua upataji wa huduma za malaria kwa watu wenye hatari kubwa zaidi. Licha ya maendeleo kidogo, watu wengi wenye hatari kubwa ya malaria bado hawapati huduma zinazoweza kuzuia, kutambua na kutibu ugonjwa huu. Changamoto katika kupanua upataji wa huduma za malaria zimeongezeka, hasa katika Afrika ya Mashariki na Kusini, kutokana na janga la COVID-19, milipuko ya kibinadamu, fedha chache, mifumo ya ufuatiliaji dhaifu, na kushuka kwa ufanisi wa zana muhimu za kupambana na malaria.

Kutokana na tishio hili na kusaidia nchi kujenga programu za malaria zenye ulinzi zaidi, WHO hivi karibuni imeangazia mbinu na muafaka mpya, ikiwa ni pamoja na: mkakati mpya wa kuzuia ulinzi wa dawa za kupambana na malaria barani Afrika; mpango mpya wa kusitisha kuenea kwa Anopheles stephensi katika mazingira ya mijini.

translations.source: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact. Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.reference: Mfumo mpya, ulioandaliwa kwa pamoja na WHO na UN-Habitat, ili kuwaongoza viongozi wa majiji katika udhibiti wa Malaria mijini. Zana mpya za kusaisdia nchi kutathmini mifumo yao ya uchunguzi wa Malaria na kutambua maeneo ya uwekezaji Pia WHO imeongeza uwazi, kubadilika na kufikia mapendekezo yake ya Malaria. Miongozo iliyounganishwa na WHO kuhusu Malaria sasa inapatikana kupitia majukwaa mawili ya kidijitaali: MAGICapp na programu ya "Malaria Toolkit". WHO inazihimiza nchi kurekebisha mapendekezo kulingana na mipangilio ya magonjwa ya eneo hilo kwa athari kubwa zaidi. Matarajio kwa afua mpya Kuendelea kwa uwekezaji katika maendeleo ya usambazaji wa chanjo mpya za Malaria na zana za kizazi kijacho itakuwa muhimu katika kufikia malengo ya kimataifa ya 2030 ya Malaria. Chanjo ya pili ya Malaria, chanjo ya R21/matrix-M (R21), kama itaidhinishwa, inaweza kusaidia kuziba pengo kubwa kati ya usambazaji na mahitaji na kupunguza zaidi ugonjwa wa watoto na kifo kutokana na Malaria. Ni kipaumbele kwa WHO kuendeleza na mapitio ya kina na madhubuti ya kitaalamu ya chanjo ya Malaria ya R21 mara Data muhimu za ziada za usalama na ufanisi kutoka kwa majaribio yanayoendelea ya awamu ya 3 ya R21 zitakazopatikana na kutolewa kwa WHO. Utangulizi wa hatua kwa hatua wa chanjo ya Malaria ya RTS,S katika nchi za ziada barani Afrika unatarajiwa kuanza mapema 2024.

translations.source: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: Katika nafasi ya kudhibiti Vekta, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya katika bomba la R&D. Zana zinazofanyiwa tathmini hujumuisha, kwa mfano, aina mpya za vyandarua vilivyotiwa dawa, chambo lengwa ambacho huvutia mbu, dawa za kuua mbu, viambata vya kuu vya nyumbani (mirija ya michirizi) na uhandisi jeni wa mbu. Watafiti wanatanguliza uendelezaji wa matibabu yasiyo ya ACT katika uwanja wa Dawa za Malaria kutokana na kuibuka na kuenea kwa ukinzani kwa sehemu ya artemisinin. Uundaji wa Dawa za kizazi kijacho, kama vile "ACT tatu", ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa Artemisinin na dawa mbili za washirika, unaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya ukinzani wa Dawa. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Kwa taarifa zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya siku ya Malaria Duniani ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day-/2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy within the context of vector control and malaria research:

Katika uwanja wa kudhibiti mbu, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya katika mchakato wa utafiti na maendeleo (R&D). Vifaa vinavyokaguliwa ni pamoja na, kwa mfano, aina mpya za vitambaa vya kulalia vilivyotendwa dawa ya mbu, chakavu kinacholengwa kinachovutia mbu, vitawimbi vinavyotimua mbu, mtego wa nyumba waumu (mafundo ya paa) na uhandisi wa jeni wa mbu. Watafiti wanakipa kipaumbele maendeleo ya matibabu yasiyo ya ACT katika uwanja wa dawa za kuzuia ugonjwa wa malaria kutokana na kuibuka na kuenea kwa upinzani wa kiasi wa artemisinin. Maendeleo ya dawa za kizazi kijacho, kama vile "ACTs tatu," ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa artemisinin na dawa mbili za mshirika, yanaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya upinzani wa dawa.

**Maelezo kwa mhariri:** Kwa habari zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya Siku ya Kimataifa ya Malaria ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Vector Control:** Translated as "kudhibiti mbu" (controlling mosquitoes).
*   **R&D Pipeline:** Translated as "mchakato wa utafiti na maendeleo" (research and development process).
*   **Insecticide-treated nets:** Translated as "vitambaa vya kulalia vilivyotendwa dawa ya mbu" (mosquito-treated sleeping nets).
*   **Targeted baits:** Translated as "chakavu kinacholengwa" (targeted bait).
*   **Spatial repellents:** Translated as "vitawimbi vinavyotimua mbu" (repellents that drive away mosquitoes).
*   **Lethal house lures (eaves tubes):** Translated as "mtego wa nyumba waumu (mafundo ya paa)" (lethal house traps (eaves tubes)).  The parenthetical explanation helps clarify the meaning of "eaves tubes" which might not be immediately obvious.
*   **Genetic engineering:** Translated as "uhandisi wa jeni" (genetic engineering).
*   **Non-ACT treatments:** Translated as "matibabu yasiyo ya ACT" (non-ACT treatments).
*   **Antimalarial medicines:** Translated as "dawa za kuzuia ugonjwa wa malaria" (medicines to prevent malaria).
*   **Triple ACTs:** Translated as "ACTs tatu" (Triple ACTs).
*   **Notes to the editor:** Translated as "Maelezo kwa mhariri" (Notes for the editor).
*   **WHO World Malaria Day campaign:** Translated as "kampeni ya Siku ya Kimataifa ya Malaria ya WHO" (WHO's International Malaria Day campaign).

I have tried to use terminology that is commonly used in Swahili within the context of public health and scientific research.  I hope this translation is helpful!

translations.source: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021. “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.reference: Mpango wa uhawilishaji wa teknolojia ya mRNA unasonga hadi hatua ijayo maendeleo yake Uzinduzi wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA Hub huko Afrige, Afrika kusini. Washirika wanakusanyika Cape Town kujadili awamu inayofuata ya ukuzaji wa chanjo ya mRNA na kubadilishana maarifa. Zaidi ya washirika 200 wa kimaifa wanaofanya kazi na mpango wa uhawilishaji wa mRNA walikutana jijini Cape Town wiki hii kwa mkutano wao wa kwanza wa ana kwa ana. Akijumuika na mkurugenzi mkuu wa shirika la afya Duniani (WHO), Dk.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus na Dk. Joel Phaahla, waziri wa afya na waziri wa biashara na viwanda, Bw. Ebrahim Patel wa Afrika Kusini pamoja na maafisa wa ngazi za juu kutoka nchi zinazofadhili mkutano wa kipekee ulikagua maendeleo tangu WHO na Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) ilipozinduliwa mwezi Juni 2021. Nina furaha kuwa hapa Cape Town na washirika wetu kusaidia mtindo endelevu wa uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA ili kuzipa nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati fursa sawa ya kupata chanjo na bidhaaa nyingine za kuokoa maisha", alisema mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ninajivunia sana mafanikio ya wale wote waliohusika katika mradi huu; kwa chini ya miaka miwili tumeonyesha kuwa tunapofanya kazi kwa ushirikiano, tunafanikiwa kwa pamoja" Washiriki wa mkutano ni pamoja na washirika wa utengenezaji wa viumbe hai kutoka nchi 15 katika mpango, wataalamu wakuu, viwanda, wawakilishi wa mashirika ya kiraia, na wafadhili. Wakati wa mkutano wa siku tano, washiriki watashiriki maendeleo na kujadili viwezeshaji muhimu vya uendelevu wa mpango kama vile masuala ya mali miliki na vipengele vya udhibiti, vilevile sayansi ya teknolojia ya mRNA na matumizi muhimu yanayohusiana na LMICs katika maeneo mengine ya ugonjwa kama vile VVU na kifua kikuu. Janga la UVIKo-19 limesisitiza kuwa ukosefu wa usawa upo katika upatikanaji wa bidha za afya, hususani chanjo.

translations.source: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network. I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.reference: Kufikia Machi 2023, zaidi ya miaka mitatu baada ya WHO kutangaza UVIKO-19 kwa dharura ya afya ya umma ya wasiwasi wa kimataifa (PHEIC), 69.7% ya watu Duniani walikuwa wamepokea angalau Dozi moja ya chanjo ya UVIKo-19. Hasa, sehemu hii bado inasalia chini ya 30% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini (LICs). Mpango huu unalenga kuchangia upatikanaji sawa wa chanjo za mRNA kwa kuongeza usambazaji wa uwezo endelevu wa utengenezaji katika LMICs, kuimarisha ushirikiano wa kikanda na baina ya kanda, na kuendeleza na kuwezesha wafanyakazi wa ndani kupitia mafunzo yaliyowekwa na kuja kujumuisha na usaidizi wa kitaalamu. Kipi cha kipekee katika kielelezo cha uhamisho wa Teknolojia ya mRNA ni mchakato wa kimataifa unaoruhusu ugavi wa Teknolojia kwa wapokeaji wengi ili kupitia uzalishaji wa ndani na kikanda wale wanaohiotaji waweze kufikiwa kwa haraka. Dk Phaahla, waziri wa afya wa Afrika Kusini, alisema : "Tunachokiona hapa leo, ni wakati katika historia, mpango ambao unalenga kuwezesha LMICs kupitia mtandao wa ushirikiano wa kimataifa. Nimefurahi kuona maendeleo yaliyofikiwa kwa muda mfupi na nakaribisha msaada kutoka kwa nchi nyingi tofauti- nchi kama Afrika Kusini ambazo zina uwezo mkubwa wa kutengeneza viumbe na ambazo ziko tayari kufanya kazi pamoja, kujifunza kutoka na kushiriki na kila mmoja". Waziri Blade Nzimande wa Elimu ya juu na Sayansi, alisema: "Letu ni dira ya mRNA Technology Transfer Hub zaidi ya UVIKo-19. Uwezo tunaojenga unatazamia kutupa ili kukabiliana na magonjwa mengine baadae ambayo chanjo zake zinaweza kutumia jukwaa la Teknolojia la mRNA. Ni Dira ambayo imeweka macho yake kuhusu magonjwa ambayo yameenea katika mazingira yetu kama vile Malaria, kifua kikuu na VVU na UKIMWI" Hafla hii pia inaashiria hatua muhimu kwa mpango kwa kuzinduliwa kwa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA Hub huko Afrigen huko Cap Town mbele ya Dk. Tedros na Dk. Matshidiso Moeti Mkurugenzi wa WHO kanda ya Afrika, Charles Gore Mkurugenzi Mtendaji wa MPP na Waheshimiwa Mawaziri walishiriki katika hafla ya kukata utepe huko Afrigen.

translations.source: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards. In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.
translations.reference: Profesa Petro Terblanche, mkurugenzi mtendaji wa Afrigen alisema: "Timu nzima ya Afrigen ina furaha kufikia hatua hii muhimu kwa kukamilika kwa jukwaa la Teknolojia ya mRNA. Jukwaa hili liko ndani ya kituo cha kutengeneza chanjo ya mwisho hadi mwisho ya mRNA ambapo mtahiniwa wa chanjo ya mRNA Hub Covid 19 AfriVac 2121 kwa sasa yuko katika hatua ya kuongeza. Katika kipindi cha miezi 18 iliyopita, Afrigen imepitia mabadiliko ya ajabu kwa usaidizi wa mtandao wa washirika na washauri wanaowezeshwa na mpango huu. Tumekuza uwezo wetu na uwezo wa kufikia viwango vya ubora wa juu zaidi vya ukuzaji wa chanjo ya mRNA, ikitumikia lengo la kujenga uwezo endelevu katika LMICs kuzalisha chanjo za mRNA". Afrigen imefanikiwa kuimarisha mchakato wa kutengeneza chanjo ya UVIKO-19 kwa kiwango cha maabara na kwa sasa inaongeza mchakato huo hadi kufikia kiwango kinachofaa kwa utengenezaji makundi ya chanjo zitakazo tumika katika majaribio ya matibabu ya awamu ya I/II. Katika mchakato sambamba, Afrigen itaendelea kutekeleza mafunzo na uhamisho wa Teknolojia kwa washirika wa mtandao. Wafadhili wanajua jukumu muhimu katika kusaidia mpango wa ufadhili wa jumla hadi sasa wa Dola za kimarekani milioni 117 na Ufaransa ikiwa ya kwanza kufadhili kazi ya uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA. Waliohudhuria mkutano huo ni wawakilishi kutoka Tume ya Ulaya, Ubelgiji, Ujerumani, vilevile Norway, Kanada, Umoja wa Afrika, Afrika Kusini, na wakfu wa ELMA. Caroline Delany, mkurugenzi mkuu wa Global Affairs Kanada kusini na mashariki mwa Afrika alisema: "Kanada inathibitisha kuendelea kuunga mkono mpango wa uhawilishaji Teknolojia wa mRNA na tuna furaha kutangaza mchango zaidi wa Dola milioni 15 za Kanada katika mkutano wa leo. Hii inaleta jumla ya mchango wa Kanada kwenye mpango hadi Dola milioni 45 za kikanada.

translations.source: These funds support the running of the Hub in South Africa as well as network partners around the world. We firmly believe in the importance of building capacity at local and regional level”. Mr Martin Seychell, Deputy Executive Director European Commission, said. “The EC has already contributed €40 million to the establishment of the mRNA technology transfer Hub and has recently signed another grant with the EIB for €15,5 million to facilitate the expansion of vaccines manufacturing capacity. This is part of the overall investment under the EU Global Gateway strategy, where more than €1 Billion have been mobilised under the Team Europe Initiative on Manufacturing health products. It is now particularly important to also address possible regulatory and demand bottlenecks, to ensure not only production of vaccines of the highest standard in all regions of the world, but also rapid and equitable access to those vaccines.”
translations.reference: Fedha hizi zinasaidia uendeshaji wa Hub nchini Afrika Kusini vilevile washirika wa mtandao Duniani kote. Tunaamini kwa dhati umuhimu wa kujenga uwezo katika ngazi ya nchi na kikanda", alisema Bw Martin Seychell, Naibu mkurugenzi mtendaji tume ya ulaya. "EC tayari imechangia yuro milioni 40 katika uanzishwaji wa kituo cha uhawilishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA na hivi karibuni imetia saini ruzuku nyingine na EIB kwa yuro milioni 15.5 kuwezesha upanuzi wa uwezo wa kutengeneza chanjo. Hii ni sehemu ya uwekezaji wa jumla ya mkakati wa EU Global Gatway, ambapo zaidi ya yuro Bilioni 1 zimekusanyw chini ya mpango wa timu ya Ulaya juu ya utengenezaji wa bidhaa za afya. Kwa sasa ni muhimu hasa kushughulikia vikwazo vinavyowezekana vya udhibiti na mahitaji, ili kuhakikisha sio tu uzalishaji wa chanjo za kiwango cha juu katika maeneo yote Duniani, lakini pia upatikanaji wa haraka na sawa wa chanjo hizo"

translations.source: Key leadership appointments made to drive WHO strategic direction and initiatives The World Health Organization (WHO) has appointed five new senior figures to its headquarters leadership team in Geneva. The new appointments follow the reappointment of Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus to his second five-year term as Director-General. The overall leadership team has been consolidated to align with the Organization’s priorities for the next five years and will work closely with the Director-General to drive forward these priorities and WHO’s ambitious transformation agenda. To accelerate progress on implementation of WHO’s 13th General Programme of Work (GPW13), and achievement of its “triple billion” targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals, WHO’s work for the coming five years is focused on five priorities: making a paradigm shift towards promoting health and preventing disease by addressing its root causes; radically reorienting health systems towards primary health care; strengthening the systems for health emergency preparedness and response; harnessing the power of science, innovation, data and technologies to advance health; and strengthening WHO as the leading authority on global health and to achieve impact in countries.
translations.reference: Uteuzi muhimu wa uongozi ulifanywa ili kusimamia muelekeo na mipango ya kimkakati ya WHO Shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) limeteua vigogo watano wapya kwa tume yake ya uongozi ya makao makuu mjini Jeniva. Uteuzi huo mpya unafuatia kuteuliwa tena kwa Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghereyesus katika muhula wake wa pili wa miaka mitano kama mkurugenzi mkuu. Timu ya jumla ya uongozi imeunganishwa ili kuendana na vipaumbele vya shirika kwa miaka mitano ijayo na itafanya kazi kwa karibu na mkurugenzi mkuu ili kuendeleza vipaumbele hivi na ajenda ya mabadiliko ya WHO. Ili kuharakisha maendeleo katika utekelezaji wa mpango mkuu wa 13 wa kazi za WHO (GPW13), kufikia malengo yake ya "Bilioni tatu" na malengo ya maendeleo endelevu yanayousiana na afya, kazi ya WHO kwa miaka mitano ijayo inazingatia vipaumbele vitano: Kufanya mabadiliko ya mtazamo kuelekea kukuza afya na kuzuia magonjwa kwa kushughulikia vyanzo vyake; Kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kuelekea huduma ya afya ya msingi Kuimarisha mifumo ya maandalizi na majibu ya dharura ya afya; Kutumia nguvu ya sayansi, uvumbuzi , Data na teknolojia ili kuendeleza afya; na Kuimarisha WHO kama mamlaka inayoongoza kwa afya Duniani na kufikia athari katika nchi.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Mabadiliko muhimu ya uongozi yafanywa ili kuongoza mwelekeo na mipango mikuu ya WHO. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) limefanya uteuzi wa viongozi wanne wapya waandamizi katika timu yake ya uongozi makao makuu mjini Geneva.  Uteuzi huu mpya unafuata kupewa upya Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus kwa muhula wa miaka mitano ya pili kama Mkurugenzi Mkuu. Timu ya uongozi kwa ujumla imeimarishwa ili kuendana na vipaumbele vya Shirika kwa miaka mitano ijayo na itafanya kazi kwa karibu na Mkurugenzi Mkuu ili kuendeleza vipaumbele hivi na ajenda ya mabadiliko ya WHO yenye malengo makubwa.  Ili kuongeza kasi ya maendeleo katika utekelezaji wa Mpango Mkuu wa Toleo la 13 wa WHO (GPW13), na kufikia malengo ya "trilioni tatu" na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu yanayohusiana na afya, kazi ya WHO kwa miaka mitano ijayo inalenga katika vipaumbele vitano: kufanya mabadiliko makubwa kuelekea kukuza afya na kuzuia ugonjwa kwa kushughulikia mizizi yake; kubadili kwa ufanisi mifumo ya afya kuelekea huduma za afya za msingi; kuimarisha mifumo ya uandaaji na majibu ya dharura za afya; kutumia nguvu ya sayansi, uvumbuzi, data na teknolojia ili kuendeleza afya; na kuimarisha WHO kama mamlaka inayoongoza katika afya ya kimataifa na kufikia athari katika nchi.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Swahili.gs vs. Standard Swahili:** I've aimed for Swahili.gs principles, which prioritize clarity and ease of understanding, often using more descriptive phrases and avoiding overly complex grammatical structures.
*   **"Key leadership appointments":** Translated as "Mabadiliko muhimu ya uongozi" (Important changes in leadership).
*   **"Strategic direction and initiatives":** Translated as "mwelekeo na mipango mikuu" (direction and main plans).
*   **"Reappointment":** Translated as "kupewa upya" (to be given anew/renewed).
*   **"Consolidated":** Translated as "imeimarishwa" (has been strengthened/reinforced).
*   **"Transformation agenda":** Translated as "ajenda ya mabadiliko" (agenda of change).  The addition of "yenye malengo makubwa" (with big goals) emphasizes the ambitious nature.
*   **"Triple billion targets":** Translated as "malengo ya trilioni tatu" (targets of trillions three).
*   **"Paradigm shift":** Translated as "mabadiliko makubwa" (big changes).
*   **"Radically reorienting":** Translated as "kubadili kwa ufanisi" (to change effectively).
*   **"Harnessing the power":** Translated as "kutumia nguvu" (to use the power).
*   **"Leading authority":** Translated as "mamlaka inayoongoza" (leading authority).
*   **Word Choice:** I've tried to use common and easily understood Swahili words.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've broken down some longer English sentences into shorter, more manageable Swahili sentences for better readability.

**Important Notes:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this document is for a very technical audience, some adjustments might be needed.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Swahili speaker, especially one familiar with the terminology used by the WHO, to ensure accuracy and naturalness.
*   **Swahili.gs Resources:**  Refer to the Swahili.gs guidelines for specific recommendations on word choice and grammar.  [https://swahiligs.github.io/](https://swahiligs.github.io/)

translations.source: The headquarters leadership team, comprising equal numbers of women and men, will work jointly with WHO regional and country offices. New members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Jeremy Farrar will become WHO’s Chief Scientist as of 8 May 2023. The appointment of Dr Farrar was previously announced in December 2022. In this role Dr Farrar will oversee the Science Division, bringing together the best experts and networks in science and innovation from around the world to guide, develop and deliver high quality health policies and services to the people who need them most. Prior to joining WHO Dr Farrar was Director of the Wellcome Trust. Before joining Wellcome in 2013 Dr Farrar spent 17 years as Director of the Clinical Research Unit at the Hospital for Tropical Diseases in Vietnam where his research interests were in global health with a focus on emerging infectious diseases. Dr Farrar is a clinician researcher with a medical degree and a PhD. Dr Ailan Li will become Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Healthier Populations as of 8 May 2023. In this role Dr Li will oversee the Organization’s efforts to promote better health and well-being through interventions relating to the environmental, social, and economic determinants of health, including climate change, tobacco control, chemical safety, road safety, food systems and nutrition, physical activity, air pollution and radiation, through a One Health approach. Dr Li has been serving as the WHO Representative to the Kingdom of Cambodia since 15 July 2019.
translations.reference: Timu ya uongozi ya makao makuu, inayojumuisha idadi sawa ya wanawake na wanaume, itafanya kazi kwa pamoja na ofisi za WHO za kikanda na nchi. Wanachama wapya wa timu ya uongozi ya makao makuu: Dk Jeremy Farrar atakuwa mwanasayansi mkuu wa WHO kuanzia tarehe 8 Mei 2023. Uteuzi wa Dk Farrar ulitangazwa hapo awali mwezi Desemba 2022. Katika jukumu hili Dk Farrar atasimamia kitengo cha sayansi, akiwaleta pamoja watalaamu wobobezi na mitandao katika sayansi na uvumbuzi kutoka Duniani kote ili kuongoza, kuendeleza na kutoa sera na huduma za afya za ubora wa juu kwa watu wanaohitaji zaidi. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO Dk Farrar alikuwa mkurugenzi wa Wellcome Trust. Kabla ya kujiunga na Wellcome mwaka 2013 Dk Farrar alitumia miaka 17 kama mkurugenzi wa kitengo cha utafiti wa Kliniki katika hospitali ya magonjwa ya kitropiki nchini Vietnamu ambapo maslahi yake ya utafiti yalikuwa katika afya ya kiamataifa kwa kuzingatia magonjwa ya kuambukiza yanajitokeza. Dk Farrar ni mtafiti wa kliniki mwenye shahada ya matibabu na shahada ya Uzamivu. Dkt Alian Li atakuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa huduma ya afya kwa wote, idadi ya watu wenye afya bora kuanzia tarehe 8 Mei 2023. Katika jukumu hili Dk Li atasimamia juhudu za shirika za kukuza afya bora na ustawi kupitia afua zinazohusu viashiria vya afya kimazingira, kijamii na kiuchumi, ikijumuisha maadiliko ya Tabianchi, udhibiti wa Tumbaku, usalama wa kemikali, usalama wa barabarani, mifumo ya chakula na lishe, shughuli za kawaida, uchafuzi wa hewa na mionzi, kupitia mbinuya Afya ya Pamoja. Dk Li amekuwa akihudumu kama mwakilishi wa WHO katika Ulaflme wa Kambodia tangu 15 julai 2019.

I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Prior to this role, Dr Li served as Regional Emergency Director for the WHO Health Emergencies Programme in the WHO Regional Office for the Western Pacific. Dr Li holds a medical degree and degree in health social sciences. Dr Yukiko Nakatani will become Assistant Director-General for Access to Medicines and Health Products as of 2 May 2023. In this role Dr Nakatani will oversee the development and implementation of WHO’s norms and policies to ensure equitable access to quality medicines, vaccines and diagnostics for all populations everywhere, including for preventing and responding to epidemics. Dr Nakatani has been serving as the Director of the Cancer and Disease Control Division of the Japanese Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare and as the Technical Advisor to the Minister of State for the COVID-19 Vaccination Task Force in Japan. Dr Nakatani holds a medical degree and a PhD in public health. Dr Razia Pendse will become the Chef de Cabinet as of 4 May 2023. In this role Dr Pendse will head the Director-General’s Office, helping to drive the Organization’s priorities and initiatives, and will ensure alignment within the WHO leadership team and across the three levels of WHO. Dr Pendse has been serving as the Director of Healthier Populations and Noncommunicable Diseases in the WHO Regional Office for South-East Asia and was previously the WHO Representative to Sri Lanka. Dr Pendse holds a medical degree and master's degree in public health.
translations.reference: Kabla ya jukumu hili, Dk Li aliwahi kuwa mkurugenzi wa Dharura wa kanda wa mpango wa dhrura wa afya wa WHO katika ofisi ya kanda ya WHO ya Pasifiki Magharibi. Dk Li ana shahada ya matibabu na shahada katika Sayansi ya afya ya kijamii. Dkt Yukiko Nakatani atakuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa upatikanaji wa dawa na bidhaa za afya kuanzia tarehe 2 Mei 2023. Katika jukumu hili Dk Nakatani atasimamaia maendeleo na utekelezaji wa kanuni na sera za WHO ili kuhakikisha upatikanaji sawa wa dawa bora, chanjo na uchunguzi kwa watu wote kila mahali, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuzuia na kukabiliana na magonjwa ya mlipuko. Dkt Nakatani amekuwa mkurugenzi wa kitengo cha kudhibiti saratani na magonjwa cha wizara ya afya, kazi na ustawi wa Japani na kama mshauri wa kiufundi wa waziri wa nchi wa kikosi kazi cha chanjo ya UVIKO-19 nchini Japani. Dk Nakatani ana shahada ya matibabu na shahada ya Uzamivu katiak afya ya umma. Dkt Razia Pendse atakuwa mkuu wa Baraza la mawaziri kuanzia tarehe 4 Mei 2023. Katika jukumu hili Dk Pendse ataongoza ofisi ya mkurugenzi mkuu, akisaidia kuendeleza vipaumbele na mipango ya shirika, atahakikisha uwiano ndani ya timu ya uongozi ya WHO na katika ngazi tatu za WHO. Dk Pendse amekuwa mkurugenzi wa idadi ya watu wenye afya bora na magonjwa yasiyoamukiza katika ofisi ya kanda ya WHO ya Kusini Mashsriki mwa Asia na hapo awali alikuwa mwakilishi wa WHO nchini Sri Lanka. Dk Pendse ana shahada ya matibabu na shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma.
translations.hypothesis: Kabla ya wadhifa huu, Dkt. Li aliwahi kuwa Mkurugenzi wa Dharura za Mkoa kwa Programu ya Dharura za Afya za WHO ofisi ya WHO Mkoa wa Pasifiki Magharibi. Dkt. Li anashikilia shahada ya matibabu na shahada katika sayansi za afya jamii. Dkt. Yukiko Nakatani atakuwa Naibu Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Upataji wa Madawa na Bidhaa za Afya kuanzia 2 Mei 2023. Katika wadhifa huu, Dkt. Nakatani atasimamia maendeleo na utekelezaji wa kanuni na sera za WHO ili kuhakikisha upataji wa madawa, chanjo na uchunguzi wa ubora kwa idadi ya watu wote kila mahali, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuzuia na kujibu magonjwa ya mlipuko. Dkt. Nakatani amekuwa akihudumu kama Mkurugenzi wa Idara ya Udhibiti wa Kansa na Magonjwa ya Wizara ya Afya, Kazi na Ustawi ya Japani na kama Mshauri wa Kiufundi kwa Waziri wa Jimbo wa Kikosi Kazi cha Chanjo ya COVID-19 nchini Japani. Dkt. Nakatani anashikilia shahada ya matibabu na shahada ya udaktari (PhD) katika afya ya jamii. Dkt. Razia Pendse atakuwa Chef de Cabinet kuanzia 4 Mei 2023. Katika wadhifa huu, Dkt. Pendse atakuwa mkuu wa Ofisi ya Mkurugenzi Mkuu, akisaidia kukuza vipaumbele na mipango ya Shirika, na kuhakikisha muunganisho ndani ya timu ya viongozi wa WHO na katika ngazi tatu za WHO. Dkt. Pendse amekuwa akihudumu kama Mkurugenzi wa Popote Zenye Afya Bora na Magonjwa ya Lisiloambukiza katika Ofisi ya WHO Mkoa wa Asia Kusini-Mashariki na hapo awali alikuwa Mwakilishi wa WHO nchini Sri Lanka. Dkt. Pendse anashikilia shahada ya matibabu na shahada ya ustaadhini katika afya ya jamii.

translations.source: Dr Jérôme Salomon will become Assistant Director-General for Universal Health Coverage, Communicable and Noncommunicable Diseases as of 17 April 2023. In this role Dr Salomon will oversee a broad portfolio of technical programmes covering HIV, viral hepatitis, sexually-transmitted infections, tuberculosis, malaria, neglected tropical diseases, mental health, substance use disorders, and noncommunicable diseases such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, chronic respiratory diseases and cancer. Dr Salomon has been serving as the Director-General for Health at the Ministry of Health and Prevention of France. He was a Member of the WHO Executive Board prior to his appointment and has extensive experience in health systems management, communicable diseases and international public health. Dr Salomon holds a medical degree, a master’s degree in public health and a PhD in epidemiology. The portfolios of existing members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Samira Asma will continue as the Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact. Dr Asma oversees a portfolio which ensures that health data are reliable and accessible, and are used to improve health outcomes worldwide, including tracking and accelerating progress towards the triple billion targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals. Prior to joining WHO in 2018, Dr Asma held leadership positions at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) for over two decades. She holds a doctorate degree in dental surgery and a master's degree in public health. Dr Bruce Aylward has been appointed Assistant Director-General of the Universal Health Coverage, Life Course Division as of 4 May 2023.
translations.reference: Dkt Jerome Solomon atakuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa huduma ya afya kwa wote, magonjwa yanayoambukiza na yasiyoamukiza kuanzia tarehe 17 Aprili 2023. Katika jukumu hili Dk Solomon atasimamia sehemu kubwa ya Programuza kiufundi zinaohusu VVU, homa ya Ini, Magonjwa ya Zinaa, kifua kikuu, Malaria, magonjwa kitropiki yaliyosahaulika, afya ya akili, matatizo ya matumizi ya dawa na magonjwa yasiyoambukiza kama vile magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, kisukari, magonjwa sugu ya kupumua na Saratani. Dk Solomon amekuwa akihudumu kama mkurugenzi mkuu wa afya katika wizara ya afya na kinga ya Ufaransa. Alikuwa mjumbe wa Halmashauri kuu ya WHO kabla ya kuteuliwa na ana uzoefu mkubwa katika usimamizi wa mifumo ya afya, magonjwa ya kuambukiza na afya ya kimataifa ya umma. Dk Solomon ana shahada ya matibabu, shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma na shahada ya uzamivu katika magonjwa. Nyadhifa za wanachama waliopo wa timu ya uongozi ya makao makuu: Dk Samira Asma ataendelea kuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa Takwimu, uchanganuzi na uwasilishaji wa Athari. Dkt Asma anasimamia idara ambayo inahakikisha kwamba Data ya afya ni ya kuaminika na inapatikana, na inatumiwa kuboresha matokeo ya afya Duniani kote, ikiwa ni pamoja na kufuatilia na kuharakisha maendeleo kuelekea malengo ya Bilioni tatu na malengo ya maendeleo endelevu yanayohusiana na Afya. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO mwaka 2018, Dk Asma alishikilia nyadhifa za uongozi katika vituo vya Marekani vya kudhibiti na kuzuia magonjwa (CDC) kwa zaidi ya miongo miwili. Ana shahada ya udaktari katika upasuaji wa meno na shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma. Dkt Bruce Aylward ameteuliwa kuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa huduma ya afya kwa wote, idara ya kozi ya maisha kuanzia tarehe 4 Mei 2023.

Salomon atasimamia programu mbalimbali za kiufundi zinazohusu UKIMWI, hepatitis virusi, maambukizi ya zinaa, kifua kikuu, ugonjwa wa malaria, magonjwa ya kijiografia yasiyojulikana, afya ya akili, matumizi ya dawa za kulevya, na magonjwa ya kisukali kama magonjwa ya moyo, ugonjwa wa kisukari, magonjwa ya kupumua sugu na saratani. Dkt. Salomon amekuwa akihudumu kama Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Afya katika Wizara ya Afya na Kuzuia Ugonjwa wa Ufaransa. Alikuwa Mwanachama wa Bodi ya Wakurugenzi ya WHO kabla ya uteuzi wake na ana uzoefu mwingi katika usimamizi wa mifumo ya afya, magonjwa yanayoweza kushushwa na afya ya umma ya kimataifa. Dkt. Salomon anashikilia shahada ya matibabu, shahada ya ustaarabu katika afya ya umma na PhD katika epidemiology. Majukumu ya washiriki wengine wa timu ya uongozi wa makao makuu: Dkt. Samira Asma ataendelea kama Msaidizi Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Takwimu, Uchambuzi na Utoaji wa Athari. Dkt. Asma anasimamia majukumu ambayo yanahakikisha kuwa data za afya ni za kuaminika na zinapatikana, na zinafanya kazi kuboresha matokeo ya afya duniani kote, ikiwa ni pamoja na kufuatilia na kuongeza kasi ya maendeleo kuelekea malengo ya trilioni tatu na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu yanayohusiana na afya. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO mnamo 2018, Dkt. Asma alishikilia nyadhifa za uongozi katika U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) kwa zaidi ya muongo mmoja. Anashikilia shahada ya udaktari katika upasuaji wa meno na shahada ya ustaarabu katika afya ya umma. Dkt. Bruce Aylward ameteuliwa kuwa Msaidizi Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Afya ya Ulimwenguni kwa Wote, Idara ya Mfumo wa Maisha kuanzia tarehe 4 Mei 2023.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Terminology:** I've used standard Swahili terms for medical and public health concepts where possible.  For example, "communicable diseases" is translated as "magonjwa yanayoweza kushushwa."
*   **Titles:**  "Dr." is translated as "Dkt."
*   **Organization Names:** I've kept the acronyms WHO and CDC in English, as they are widely recognized internationally.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've aimed for a natural flow in Swahili, sometimes rephrasing slightly to ensure the meaning is clear.
*   **"Portfolio":** The word "portfolio" is translated as "majukumu" which means responsibilities or duties.
*   **"Triple Billion Targets":** This is translated as "malengo ya trilioni tatu" which is a direct translation.

translations.source: Dr Aylward has been leading WHO’s Transformation Agenda and the Organization’s work on the multi-agency Access to COVID-19 Tools Accelerator (ACT-A) Hub. In his new role, Dr Aylward will drive the Organization’s agenda to transform primary health care as central to universal health coverage, as well as overseeing WHO’s work on health systems, immunization and reproductive, maternal and child health. Dr Aylward holds a medical degree and a master’s degree in public health.Dr Hanan Balkhy will continue as Assistant Director-General for Antimicrobial Resistance. Dr Balkhy provides technical and political leadership to curb the health and economic burdens of drug resistance, including through multi-sectoral collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization, the World Organization for Animal Health, and the United Nations Environment Programme. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Balkhy was Executive Director for Infection Prevention and Control at Saudi Arabia’s Ministry of National Guard. Dr Balkhy holds a medical degree with specialization in paediatric infectious diseases. Dr Catharina Boehme will become Assistant Director-General, External Relations and Governance as of 4 May 2023. In this role Dr Boehme will lead WHO’s strategic engagement in the areas of governance, resource mobilization and partner relations. Her portfolio will include critical Member States processes, such as their negotiation of a pandemic accord, the reform of WHO’s governance and the implementation of recommendations on sustainable financing. Dr Boehme has been serving as the Director-General’s Chef de Cabinet during which time she drove the leadership’s strategic vision, ensuring alignment across the Organization and with Member States and partners.
translations.reference: Dk Aylward amekuwa akiongoza ajenda ya mabadiliko ya WHO na kazi ya shirika kwenye kitovu cha wakala mbalimbali cha ufikiaji wa vifaa vya kuharakisha zana za UVIKO-19 (ACT-A). Ktika jukumu lake jipya, Dk Aylward ataendesha ajenda ya shirika la kubadilisha huduma ya afya ya msingi kama msingi wa huduma ya afya kwa wote, vilevile kusimamia kazi ya WHO kuhusu mifumo ya afya, chanjo na afya ya uzazi, uzazi na mtoto. Dkt Aylward ana shahada ya udaktari na shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma. Dk Balky ataendelea kuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa upinzani wa Antimikrobia. Dk Balkhy hutoa uongozi wa kiufundi na kisiasa ili kupunguza mizigo ya kiafya na kiuchumi ya ukinzani wa Dawa, ikijumuisha kupitia ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali na shirika la chakula na kilimo, shirika la afya ya wanyama Duniani, na mpango wa mazingira wa umoja wa mataifa. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO, Dk Balkhy alikuwa mkurugenzi mtendaji wa kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi katika wizara ya walinzi wa kitaifa ya Saudi Arabia. Dk Balkhy ana shahada ya matibabu na utaalamu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kwa watoto. Dk Catharina Boehme atakuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi, mahusiano ya kimataifa na utawala kuanzia tarehe 4 Mei 2023. Katika jukumu hili Dk Boehme ataongoza ushiriki wa kimkakati wa WHO katika maeneo ya utawala, uhamishaji wa rasilimali na uhusiano wa washirika. Jarada lake litajumuisha michakato muhimu ya nchi wanachama, kama vile mazungumzo yao ya makubaliano ya janga, mageuzi ya utawala wa WHO na utekelezaji wa mapendekezo juu ya ufadhili endelevu. Dk Boehme amekuwa akihudumu kama mkuu wa Baraza la mawaziri la mkurugenzi mkuu wakati ambapo aliongoza maono ya kimkakati ya uongozi, kuhakikisha uwiano katika shirika na nchi wanachama na washirika.

translations.source: She joined WHO in 2021, having been the Chief Executive Officer of FIND, the international alliance for diagnostics. Dr Boehme holds a medical degree and diplomas in public health and management. Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu will continue as Assistant Director-General for the Division of Health Emergency Intelligence and Surveillance Systems in the Emergencies Programme. As part of his portfolio, Dr Ihekweazu leads the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, bringing together partners to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, analytical capacities, and tools for decision-making. Prior to joining WHO, Dr Ihekweazu was the first Director General of the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC), from 2016 to 2021. Dr Ihekweazu holds a medical degree, with specialization as an infectious disease epidemiologist, and a master’s degree in public health. Dr Michael Ryan will continue as Executive Director of WHO’s Health Emergencies Programme. Dr Ryan has been managing health emergencies in WHO for the past 25 years and most recently led WHO’s emergency response to the COVID-19 pandemic. He leads WHO’s response to disease outbreaks, humanitarian crises and other public health emergencies. Dr Ryan is a founding member of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN), which has aided the response to hundreds of disease outbreaks around the world.
translations.reference: Alijiunga na WHO mnamo 2021, akiwa Afisa mkuu mtendaji wa FIND, muungano wa kimataifa wa uchunguzi. Dk Boehme ana shahada ya matibabu na Stashahada katika afya ya umma na usimamizi. Dk Chikwe Ihekweazu ataendelea kuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa kitengo cha mifumo ya upelelzi wa dharura ya kiafya na ufuatiliaji katika mpango wa dharura. Kama sehemu ya jalada lake, Dk Ihekweazu anaongoza kitovu cha WHO cha ujasusi wa ugonjwa wa mlipuko na milipuko, akiwaleta pamoja washirika kushughulikia hatari za janga na magonjwa ya siku siku zijazo na ufikiaji bora wa Data, uwezo wa uchambuzi, na zana za kufanya maamuzi. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO, Dk Ihekweazu alikuwa mkurugenzi mkuu wa kwanza wa kituo cha kudhibiti magonjwa cha Nigeria (NCDC), kuanzia 2016 hadi 2021. Dk Ihekweazu ana shshada ya matibabu, na utaalamu kama mtaalamu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza, na shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma. Dtk Michael Ryan ataendelea kama mkurugenzi mtendaji wa mpango wa dharura wa afya wa WHO. Dk Ryan amekuwa akisimamia dharura za kiafya katika WHO kwa miaka 25 iliyopita na hivi karibuni aliongoza makabiliano ya dharura ya WHO kwa janga la UVIKO-19. Anaongoza muitikio wa WHO kwa milipuko ya magonjwa, majanga ya kibinadamu na dharura nyingine za afya ya umma. Dk Ryan mwanachama mwanzilishi wa mtandao wa tahadhari na makabiliano ya mlipuko wa ulimwenguni (GOARN), ambao umesaidia kukabiliana na mamia ya milipuko ya magonjwa kote ulimwenguni.

translations.source: Dr Ryan holds a degree in medicine and a master’s degree in public health. Mr Raul Thomas will continue as Assistant Director-General for Business Operations. Mr Thomas has overall responsibility for WHO’s business functions, including budget, finance, human resources, administration, audit, risk management, accountability and compliance, and general management. He is responsible for implementing many of the initiatives under the Organization’s transformation agenda and creating a respectful, inclusive workplace. During his 20 years of employment with WHO, Mr Thomas has served in the regions of Africa, the Americas, the Eastern Mediterranean and the Western Pacific, as well as with the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC). Mr Thomas holds a master’s degree in organizational management and a bachelor’s degree in business administration.
translations.reference: Dk Ryan ana shahada ya udaktari na shahada ya uzamili katika afya ya umma. Bw Raul Thomas ataendelea kuwa mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa uendeshaji Biashara. Bw. Thomas ana wajibu wa jumla wa kazi biashara za WHO, ikiwa ni pamoja na bajeti, fedha, rasilimali watu, utawala, ukaguzi, usimamizi wa hatari, uwajibikaji na utii, na usimamizi wa jumla. Ana jukumu la kutekeleza mipango mingi chini ya ajenda ya mabadiliko ya shirika na kuunda mahali pakazi pa heshima na jumuishi. Katika miaka yake 20 ya ajira na WHO, Bw Thomas amehudumu katika kanda za Afrika, Mashariki ya Mediterania na Pasifiki Magharibi, vilevile shirika la kimataifa la utafiti wa saratani (IARC). Bw. Thomas ana shahada ya uzamili katika usimamizi wa shirika na shahada ya awali katika usimamizi wa Biashara.
